name: ai-ml
description: AI and machine learning workflow covering LLM application development, RAG implementation, agent architecture, ML pipelines, and AI-powered features.
capabilities: Guide users through 7 phases of AI application development
Provide skill orchestration for LLM, RAG, agent, and ML pipeline development
Offer copy-paste prompts for invoking specialized skills
Include development checklists for LLM integration, RAG systems, and AI agents
Define quality gates for AI feature development
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 75 potential issues (external_commands, weak_crypto, system_reconnaissance) but all are false positives. The file is a markdown documentation bundle that orchestrates other skills via reference names in code blocks. No actual code execution, shell commands, or cryptographic operations exist. This is safe for publication.
valueStatement: This workflow bundle provides a comprehensive guide for building production AI applications, from LLM integration to RAG systems and AI agents. It orchestrates multiple specialized skills into a cohesive development process.
limitations: This is a workflow orchestration guide, not executable code
Requires other skills to be available for full functionality
Does not implement AI models directly - it references other skills
Does not include actual code implementations

useCases.title: Building LLM-powered applications
useCases.description: Follow the phased workflow to design, integrate, and deploy LLM-powered features with proper observability.

useCases.title: Implementing RAG systems
useCases.description: Use the RAG implementation phase to set up vector databases, embedding strategies, and retrieval pipelines.

useCases.title: Creating AI agent systems
useCases.description: Design multi-agent architectures using autonomous agent patterns, CrewAI, and LangGraph integration.

promptTemplates.title: Design AI Product Feature
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @ai-product to design AI-powered features for my application. Follow the Phase 1 workflow.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new AI feature project

promptTemplates.title: Build RAG Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @rag-engineer to design RAG pipeline, then @vector-database-engineer to set up vector search, and @embedding-strategies to select optimal embeddings.

promptTemplates.title: Create Multi-Agent System
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @crewai to build role-based multi-agent system, then @langgraph to create stateful AI workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Set up ML Pipeline
promptTemplates.prompt: Use @ml-engineer to build machine learning pipeline and @mlops-engineer to set up MLOps infrastructure.

bestPractices: Follow the workflow phases in order for comprehensive AI development
Use the checklist items to ensure all critical components are addressed
Invoke specialized skills for deep expertise in each area
Apply quality gates before deploying AI features to production
antiPatterns: Skipping workflow phases - each phase builds on previous work
Ignoring the observability phase - AI systems need monitoring
Not following security practices - AI features require input validation and rate limiting
Skipping quality gates - AI features need thorough testing before deployment

faq.question: What is the AI/ML Workflow Bundle?
faq.answer: This is a comprehensive workflow guide that orchestrates multiple AI/ML skills into 7 phases: AI Application Design, LLM Integration, RAG Implementation, AI Agent Development, ML Pipeline Development, AI Observability, and AI Security.

faq.question: Do I need other skills installed?
faq.answer: Yes, this workflow references many other skills like ai-product, rag-engineer, crewai, langgraph, and ml-engineer. These skills must be available for the prompts to work.

faq.question: Can I skip workflow phases?
faq.answer: While you can adapt the workflow to your needs, skipping phases may result in incomplete implementation. The phases are designed to build on each other.

faq.question: What tools does this support?
faq.answer: This workflow supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The skill references work across these AI coding assistants.

faq.question: Does this include executable code?
faq.answer: No, this is a workflow orchestration guide in markdown format. It provides prompts and checklists, not actual implementation code.

faq.question: How do I get started?
faq.answer: Start with Phase 1: AI Application Design. Define your use cases, choose appropriate models, and design your system architecture before moving to implementation phases.
